marketState: neutral-to-bullish: weather-led near-term strength capped by storage surplus and stretched valuation
summary: NG front trades at $3.276, modestly above its seasonal median path (+4.2pp vs median) and above modeled fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) at every horizon out to 21 days, though the front-fair gap narrows over time (spread +$0.347 now shrinking to +$0.185 by +21D) as fair value itself rises into the cooling season. The dominant driver is weather: 8-16 day cooling demand is running +28.3% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, a genuinely strong bullish tailwind for summer gas burn, while the nearer 1-7 day window is milder (+11.2%, within range). Seasonality direction agrees (56/50/56/63% odds of higher prices over 1-4 weeks, +2.3% typical 4-week move) but reliability is explicitly flagged LOW (51pp spread), so this should not be leaned on alone. Storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+87 Bcf actual vs +74 Bcf forecast), a real bearish headwind, while managed-money is net short (-64,808 contracts) but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, leaving room for short-covering if the heat verifies. Technicals are split by timeframe: intraday frames (5m-4h) are bullish with healthy RSI, but daily and weekly both show a bearish 50<200 EMA structure, making the immediate 1-10 day path inconclusive.

longTerm.entry: $3.15 - $3.30 (scale in on any dip toward $3.15-3.20, or current market)
longTerm.rationale: The 8-16 day cooling-demand signal is unusually strong (+28.3% vs 5y, above the historical max range), seasonality direction agrees (63% odds higher in 4 weeks) and price is already running above its seasonal median path, all pointing to a firmer summer demand backdrop; fair value itself is projected to rise from $2.929 now toward $3.091 by +21D, narrowing the current overvaluation gap. Managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, so a hot-weather verification could trigger short-covering on top of fresh demand.
longTerm.invalidation: A shift to below-normal 8-16D cooling demand, a run of bearish storage surprises that pushes the 5y surplus meaningfully wider, or a daily/weekly close back below the $2.90-3.00 fair-value zone would invalidate the thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Front is already priced at SLIGHTLY OVER fair value with only about $0.15 of room left to the top of the current fair-value band ($3.429), and technical timeframes disagree: 5m-4h trend and EMA structure are bullish, but daily and weekly both show a bearish 50<200 EMA cross, so there is no clean directional edge over a 1-10 day window. The front-to-next backwardation (-$0.094/-$0.104 into Sep/Oct) is a normal near-term roll, not a strong signal.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $3.00-$3.10 zone (near/at modeled fair value) to buy, or a daily close above $3.43 (top of the current fair-value band) confirming a genuine breakout before entering.

keyDrivers: 8-16 day cooling demand +28.3% vs 5y and above the historical max range - the single strongest bullish signal in the data
Seasonality mildly bullish (63% odds higher in 4 weeks, +2.3% typical move) but reliability explicitly flagged LOW
Storage +175 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish build surprise - a real supply-side headwind
Managed money net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5y range, leaving room for short-covering
Front price sits SLIGHTLY OVER fair value at every modeled horizon (now through +21D), though the gap is narrowing
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly LOW (51pp 10-90% spread) - the model itself doubts this signal
A cooler-than-forecast shift in the 8-16D weather outlook or continued bearish storage surprises could remove the main bullish catalyst
Daily/weekly technical structure (50<200 EMA, weekly RSI 46) conflicts with intraday bullish momentum, raising reversal risk on the short-term trade
